Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the surface area of the volume generated when the following curves revolve around the $$y \text{-axis} \text{.}$$ If you cannot evaluate the integral exactly, use your calculator to approximate it.
Question: The base of a lamp is constructed by revolving a quarter circle $$y = \sqrt{2 x - x^{2}}$$ around the $$y \text{-axis}$$ from $$x = 1$$ to $$x = 2 ,$$ as seen here. Create an integral for the surface area of this curve and compute it.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the surface area of the volume generated when the following curves revolve around the $$y \text{-axis} \text{.}$$ If you cannot evaluate the integral exactly, use your calculator to approximate it.
Question: A light bulb is a sphere with radius $$1 \text{/} 2$$ in. with the bottom sliced off to fit exactly onto a cylinder of radius $$1 \text{/} 4$$ in. and length $$1 \text{/} 3$$ in., as seen here. The sphere is cut off at the bottom to fit exactly onto the cylinder, so the radius of the cut is $$1 \text{/} 4$$ in. Find the surface area (not including the top or bottom of the cylinder).

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the surface area of the volume generated when the following curves revolve around the $$y \text{-axis} \text{.}$$ If you cannot evaluate the integral exactly, use your calculator to approximate it.
Question: [T] A lampshade is constructed by rotating $$y = 1 \text{/} x$$ around the $$x \text{-axis}$$ from $$y = 1$$ to $$y = 2 ,$$ as seen here. Determine how much material you would need to construct this lampshade-that is, the surface area-accurate to four decimal places.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the surface area of the volume generated when the following curves revolve around the $$y \text{-axis} \text{.}$$ If you cannot evaluate the integral exactly, use your calculator to approximate it.
Question: [T] An anchor drags behind a boat according to the function $$y = 24 e^{\text{unicode[:-]} x \text{/} 2} - 24 ,$$ where $$y$$ represents the depth beneath the boat and $$x$$ is the horizontal distance of the anchor from the back of the boat. If the anchor is $$23$$ ft below the boat, how much rope do you have to pull to reach the anchor? Round your answer to three decimal places.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the surface area of the volume generated when the following curves revolve around the $$y \text{-axis} \text{.}$$ If you cannot evaluate the integral exactly, use your calculator to approximate it.
Question: [T] You are building a bridge that will span $$10$$ ft. You intend to add decorative rope in the shape of $$y = 5 | \text{sin} ( ( x \pi ) \text{/} 5 ) | ,$$ where $$x$$ is the distance in feet from one end of the bridge. Find out how much rope you need to buy, rounded to the nearest foot.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: $$y = \text{ln} ( \text{sin} x )$$ from $$x = \pi \text{/} 4$$ to $$x = ( 3 \pi ) \text{/} 4 .$$ ( Hint: Recall trigonometric identities.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: Draw graphs of $$y = x^{2} ,$$ $$y = x^{6} ,$$ and $$y = x^{10} .$$ For $$y = x^{n} ,$$ as $$n$$ increases, formulate a prediction on the arc length from $$( 0 , 0 )$$ to $$( 1 , 1 ) .$$ Now, compute the lengths of these three functions and determine whether your prediction is correct.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: Compare the lengths of the parabola $$x = y^{2}$$ and the line $$x = b y$$ from $$( 0 , 0 ) \text{to} ( b^{2} , b )$$ as $$b$$ increases. What do you notice?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: Solve for the length of $$x = y^{2}$$ from $$( 0 , 0 ) \text{to} ( 1 , 1 ) .$$ Show that $$x = ( 1 \text{/} 2 ) y^{2}$$ from $$( 0 , 0 )$$ to $$( 2 , 2 )$$ is twice as long. Graph both functions and explain why this is so.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: [T] Which is longer between $$( 1 , 1 )$$ and $$( 2 , 1 \text{/} 2 ) \text{:}$$ the hyperbola $$y = 1 \text{/} x$$ or the graph of $$x + 2 y = 3 ?$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the exact arc length for the following problems over the given interval.
Question: Explain why the surface area is infinite when $$y = 1 \text{/} x$$ is rotated around the $$x \text{-axis}$$ for $$1 \le x < \infty ,$$ but the volume is finite.
Answer: For more information, look up Gabriel’s Horn.
